title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The barrel kept exhaling. With the US-Iran stand-down holding a second day and Hormuz traffic crawling back, Brent settled near $73.40, consolidating lower as the war-risk premium drained without fully collapsing. The tells stay in war-risk insurance and AIS routing, not the headline barrel, because a single new tanker or base incident would re-arm the premium overnight.

Chips were bought, not sold. The AI hardware complex was bid hard into quarter-end, with Nvidia, AMD and the TSMC ADR all higher and US chip stocks up sharply on the quarter, even as hyperscaler capital spending approached the $1 trillion mark for 2026. The repricing risk now sits less in any single guide than in whether cheaper inference erodes the spend-at-any-cost story.

The efficiency squeeze has a flag on it. China's open-weight models kept closing the coding gap at a fraction of the price as Washington gated its strongest systems, and open-weight share on a major routing service climbed sharply. If buyers optimise for intelligence per dollar and runnable weights, capability containment matters less than who ships deployable models first.

items.why: US and Iranian delegations are in Qatar on the memorandum, but the two capitals describe the contact differently. Whether the sessions harden into a deconfliction-and-inspection track or buy time before the next incident turns on this round.

items.what: Brent · the floor, not the spike
items.why: The war premium has largely drained, but a single tanker or base strike would re-arm it overnight. The market is pricing resumed transit while keeping war-risk cover on, so watch insurance quotes and AIS more than the screen.

With Washington routing its strongest models to approved customers and China's open weights closing the coding gap, the question is whether gating contains capability or just cedes the open field while slowing US institutions.
After cross-border strikes and a summons in Kabul, the tit-for-tat cycle is open again; whether mediators can cap it before a larger operation is the live question on the eastern frontier.
Record heat is cutting nuclear output on warm rivers and lifting spot power; the read-through runs to gas demand, crop stress and labour productivity across the continent.

items.what: Washington moved to begin a decade-long countdown to exit the USMCA pact, opening a sunset-review clock that injects fresh uncertainty into North American supply chains and the peso.

The AI build-out kept defying gravity into quarter-end, with US chip stocks up roughly 75% on the quarter and 2026 hyperscaler capital spending tracking toward $1 trillion despite mounting return-on-investment scrutiny.

Bloom Energy and Brookfield expanded a multibillion-dollar partnership to supply on-site power for AI data centres, a reminder that electricity, not only chips, is the binding constraint on the compute build-out.
